text:
There once was a wise old owl perched in a towering oak. Every morning he would flap his wings and whistle the ancient melody that told tales of times long past.

One serene morning, he noticed a young squirrel rummaging through the treetops. The owl decided to engage.

"I remember," began the wise old owl.

The squirrel paused anxiously.

"In days before humans? You've heard those stories, haven't you?"

"You have stories, don't you?" asked the curious squirrel.

"All about tiny creatures," said the owl. "Like me?"

"Almost like legends!" cheered the squirrel. "How about this? Once upon a quiet dawn..."

(The forest awakens.)

"...There was no nest. Just a wide open field. The sky was a tapestry of dawn light. And there, nestled among the blades, was a mouse."

The squirrel listened carefully.

"The mouse didn't scurry away. It chirped and waved."

For some time, nothing happened.
